Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} Kokufu-chō Murayama 626 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（candidate shrine） |創建= |例祭=September 15 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Tenman Shrine''' (てんまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}) in {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is commonly called '''Murayama Tenjin'''. === Outline and history === The founding year is unknown. When the army of {{ill|lt=Anejikōji Tadamori|Q56346475|WD=Q56346475|1=Anejikōji Tadamori}}, a mid-ranking {{ill|lt=court noble|Q1196276|WD=Q1196276|1=Kuge}} and military leader in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, was defeated in battle, an image of {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} handed down in his family was enshrined here, and since then the shrine has drawn worship as Tenjingū, deity of learning. However, it is also said that earlier, Hida no Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyatsuki-no-Mikoto first enshrined Amatsukami here, and thus it came to be a joint enshrinement with Amatsukami. It is also recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} (with a possible scribal confusion of Ōtsu/Tsu). On the {{ill|lt=river terrace|Q1069618|WD=Q1069618|1=Fluvial terrace}} around the shrine is the buried-cultural-property site “Murayama ruins.” In the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} is a “Jōmon pit-dwelling site,” remains of a {{ill|lt=pit-house|Q1087793|WD=Q1087793|1=Indicator (genus)}}, designated a Gifu Prefecture {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-15|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7296.html|title=Jōmon pit-dwelling remains |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-10-19}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *Tatehayatama-no-Kami *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === Cultural property (historic site) === *'''Jōmon pit-dwelling remains''' - The '''Murayama ruins''' around the shrine were excavated in {{ill|lt=1951|Q18591|WD=Q18591|1=1951}} (Shōwa 26){{Sfn|Ōno|Shioya|1960}}, the first case in Gifu where the full structure of a {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pit-house|1=UNKNOWN}} was clarified. From the {{ill|lt=site remains|Q814254|WD=Q814254|1=Feature (archaeology)}}, about 9,700 pottery sherds (estimated 1,118+ vessels) and 200 stone tools were found. Under the name “Jōmon pit-dwelling remains,” it was designated a prefectural {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} on February 24, {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31). === Transportation === About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kamieda Station|Q5358338|WD=Q5358338|1=Hozue Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|first=Masao|last=Ōno|first2=Masao|last2=Shioya|title=Murayama Ruins|year=1960|date=1960-11|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94471|publisher=Sumihirozō|ncid=BA34956565|ref=harv}} *''Hida no Kokufu: Historical Volume'' (published by Kokufu Town Board of Education) *''History of Kokufu Town / Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties Volume'', etc. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} === External links === * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1307&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tenman Shrine - Official website of Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定史跡]]

On the {{ill|lt=river terrace|Q1069618|WD=Q1069618|1=Fluvial terrace}} around the shrine is the buried-cultural-property site “Murayama ruins.” In the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} is a “Jōmon pit-dwelling site,” remains of a {{ill|lt=pit-house|Q1087793|WD=Q1087793|1=Indicator (genus)}}, designated a Gifu Prefecture {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-15|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7296.html|title=Jōmon pit-dwelling remains |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-10-19}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *Tatehayatama-no-Kami *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === Cultural property (historic site) === *'''Jōmon pit-dwelling remains''' - The '''Murayama ruins''' around the shrine were excavated in {{ill|lt=1951|Q18591|WD=Q18591|1=1951}} (Shōwa 26){{Sfn|Ōno|Shioya|1960}}, the first case in Gifu where the full structure of a {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pit-house|1=UNKNOWN}} was clarified. From the {{ill|lt=site remains|Q814254|WD=Q814254|1=Feature (archaeology)}}, about 9,700 pottery sherds (estimated 1,118+ vessels) and 200 stone tools were found. Under the name “Jōmon pit-dwelling remains,” it was designated a prefectural {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} on February 24, {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31). === Transportation === About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kamieda Station|Q5358338|WD=Q5358338|1=Hozue Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|first=Masao|last=Ōno|first2=Masao|last2=Shioya|title=Murayama Ruins|year=1960|date=1960-11|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94471|publisher=Sumihirozō|ncid=BA34956565|ref=harv}} *''Hida no Kokufu: Historical Volume'' (published by Kokufu Town Board of Education) *''History of Kokufu Town / Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties Volume'', etc. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} === External links === * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1307&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tenman Shrine - Official website of Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県指定史跡]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} Kokufu-chō Murayama 626 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（candidate shrine） |創建= |例祭=September 15 |神事= |本殿= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Tenman Shrine''' (てんまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}}) in {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is commonly called '''Murayama Tenjin'''. === Outline and history === The founding year is unknown. When the army of {{ill|lt=Anejikōji Tadamori|Q56346475|WD=Q56346475|1=Anejikōji Tadamori}}, a mid-ranking {{ill|lt=court noble|Q1196276|WD=Q1196276|1=Kuge}} and military leader in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, was defeated in battle, an image of {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} handed down in his family was enshrined here, and since then the shrine has drawn worship as Tenjingū, deity of learning. However, it is also said that earlier, Hida no Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyatsuki-no-Mikoto first enshrined Amatsukami here, and thus it came to be a joint enshrinement with Amatsukami. It is also recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} (with a possible scribal confusion of Ōtsu/Tsu). On the {{ill|lt=river terrace|Q1069618|WD=Q1069618|1=Fluvial terrace}} around the shrine is the buried-cultural-property site “Murayama ruins.” In the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} is a “Jōmon pit-dwelling site,” remains of a {{ill|lt=pit-house|Q1087793|WD=Q1087793|1=Indicator (genus)}}, designated a Gifu Prefecture {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-15|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7296.html|title=Jōmon pit-dwelling remains |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-10-19}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *Tatehayatama-no-Kami *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === Cultural property (historic site) === *'''Jōmon pit-dwelling remains''' - The '''Murayama ruins''' around the shrine were excavated in {{ill|lt=1951|Q18591|WD=Q18591|1=1951}} (Shōwa 26){{Sfn|Ōno|Shioya|1960}}, the first case in Gifu where the full structure of a {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pit-house|1=UNKNOWN}} was clarified. From the {{ill|lt=site remains|Q814254|WD=Q814254|1=Feature (archaeology)}}, about 9,700 pottery sherds (estimated 1,118+ vessels) and 200 stone tools were found. Under the name “Jōmon pit-dwelling remains,” it was designated a prefectural {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} on February 24, {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31). === Transportation === About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kamieda Station|Q5358338|WD=Q5358338|1=Hozue Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|first=Masao|last=Ōno|first2=Masao|last2=Shioya|title=Murayama Ruins|year=1960|date=1960-11|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94471|publisher=Sumihirozō|ncid=BA34956565|ref=harv}} *''Hida no Kokufu: Historical Volume'' (published by Kokufu Town Board of Education) *''History of Kokufu Town / Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties Volume'', etc. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} === External links === * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1307&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tenman Shrine - Official website of Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gifu Prefecture]]

On the {{ill|lt=river terrace|Q1069618|WD=Q1069618|1=Fluvial terrace}} around the shrine is the buried-cultural-property site “Murayama ruins.” In the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} is a “Jōmon pit-dwelling site,” remains of a {{ill|lt=pit-house|Q1087793|WD=Q1087793|1=Indicator (genus)}}, designated a Gifu Prefecture {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-15|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7296.html|title=Jōmon pit-dwelling remains |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-10-19}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *Tatehayatama-no-Kami *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === Cultural property (historic site) === *'''Jōmon pit-dwelling remains''' - The '''Murayama ruins''' around the shrine were excavated in {{ill|lt=1951|Q18591|WD=Q18591|1=1951}} (Shōwa 26){{Sfn|Ōno|Shioya|1960}}, the first case in Gifu where the full structure of a {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pit-house|1=UNKNOWN}} was clarified. From the {{ill|lt=site remains|Q814254|WD=Q814254|1=Feature (archaeology)}}, about 9,700 pottery sherds (estimated 1,118+ vessels) and 200 stone tools were found. Under the name “Jōmon pit-dwelling remains,” it was designated a prefectural {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} on February 24, {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31). === Transportation === About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kamieda Station|Q5358338|WD=Q5358338|1=Hozue Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|first=Masao|last=Ōno|first2=Masao|last2=Shioya|title=Murayama Ruins|year=1960|date=1960-11|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94471|publisher=Sumihirozō|ncid=BA34956565|ref=harv}} *''Hida no Kokufu: Historical Volume'' (published by Kokufu Town Board of Education) *''History of Kokufu Town / Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties Volume'', etc. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} === External links === * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1307&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tenman Shrine - Official website of Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gifu Prefecture]]

It is commonly called '''Murayama Tenjin'''. === Outline and history === The founding year is unknown. When the army of {{ill|lt=Anejikōji Tadamori|Q56346475|WD=Q56346475|1=Anejikōji Tadamori}}, a mid-ranking {{ill|lt=court noble|Q1196276|WD=Q1196276|1=Kuge}} and military leader in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, was defeated in battle, an image of {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} handed down in his family was enshrined here, and since then the shrine has drawn worship as Tenjingū, deity of learning. However, it is also said that earlier, Hida no Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyatsuki-no-Mikoto first enshrined Amatsukami here, and thus it came to be a joint enshrinement with Amatsukami. It is also recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} (with a possible scribal confusion of Ōtsu/Tsu). On the {{ill|lt=river terrace|Q1069618|WD=Q1069618|1=Fluvial terrace}} around the shrine is the buried-cultural-property site “Murayama ruins.” In the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} is a “Jōmon pit-dwelling site,” remains of a {{ill|lt=pit-house|Q1087793|WD=Q1087793|1=Indicator (genus)}}, designated a Gifu Prefecture {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-15|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7296.html|title=Jōmon pit-dwelling remains |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-10-19}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *Tatehayatama-no-Kami *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === Cultural property (historic site) === *'''Jōmon pit-dwelling remains''' - The '''Murayama ruins''' around the shrine were excavated in {{ill|lt=1951|Q18591|WD=Q18591|1=1951}} (Shōwa 26){{Sfn|Ōno|Shioya|1960}}, the first case in Gifu where the full structure of a {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pit-house|1=UNKNOWN}} was clarified. From the {{ill|lt=site remains|Q814254|WD=Q814254|1=Feature (archaeology)}}, about 9,700 pottery sherds (estimated 1,118+ vessels) and 200 stone tools were found. Under the name “Jōmon pit-dwelling remains,” it was designated a prefectural {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} on February 24, {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31). === Transportation === About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kamieda Station|Q5358338|WD=Q5358338|1=Hozue Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|first=Masao|last=Ōno|first2=Masao|last2=Shioya|title=Murayama Ruins|year=1960|date=1960-11|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94471|publisher=Sumihirozō|ncid=BA34956565|ref=harv}} *''Hida no Kokufu: Historical Volume'' (published by Kokufu Town Board of Education) *''History of Kokufu Town / Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties Volume'', etc. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} === External links === * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1307&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tenman Shrine - Official website of Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gifu Prefecture]]

However, it is also said that earlier, Hida no Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyatsuki-no-Mikoto first enshrined Amatsukami here, and thus it came to be a joint enshrinement with Amatsukami. It is also recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} (with a possible scribal confusion of Ōtsu/Tsu). On the {{ill|lt=river terrace|Q1069618|WD=Q1069618|1=Fluvial terrace}} around the shrine is the buried-cultural-property site “Murayama ruins.” In the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}} is a “Jōmon pit-dwelling site,” remains of a {{ill|lt=pit-house|Q1087793|WD=Q1087793|1=Indicator (genus)}}, designated a Gifu Prefecture {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-15|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7296.html|title=Jōmon pit-dwelling remains |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2022-10-19}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsukami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *Tatehayatama-no-Kami *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === Cultural property (historic site) === *'''Jōmon pit-dwelling remains''' - The '''Murayama ruins''' around the shrine were excavated in {{ill|lt=1951|Q18591|WD=Q18591|1=1951}} (Shōwa 26){{Sfn|Ōno|Shioya|1960}}, the first case in Gifu where the full structure of a {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pit-house|1=UNKNOWN}} was clarified. From the {{ill|lt=site remains|Q814254|WD=Q814254|1=Feature (archaeology)}}, about 9,700 pottery sherds (estimated 1,118+ vessels) and 200 stone tools were found. Under the name “Jōmon pit-dwelling remains,” it was designated a prefectural {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}} on February 24, {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31). === Transportation === About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kamieda Station|Q5358338|WD=Q5358338|1=Hozue Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Central|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === *{{Cite book|和書|first=Masao|last=Ōno|first2=Masao|last2=Shioya|title=Murayama Ruins|year=1960|date=1960-11|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94471|publisher=Sumihirozō|ncid=BA34956565|ref=harv}} *''Hida no Kokufu: Historical Volume'' (published by Kokufu Town Board of Education) *''History of Kokufu Town / Archaeology and Designated Cultural Properties Volume'', etc. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)|Q11437362|WD=Q11437362|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|1=Ōtsu Shrine (Hida)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} === External links === * [http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1307&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Tenman Shrine - Official website of Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenman Shrine (Takayama)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gifu Prefecture]]
